def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a written work into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Clean object values - remove quotes from strings
    def clean_value(val: str) -> str:
        if val.startswith('"') and val.endswith('"'):
            return val[1:-1]
        return val

    # Build a mapping of subjects to their triples
    subject_triples = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[t.subject] = []
        subject_triples[t.subject].append(t)

    # Determine the main subject (first triple's subject)
    main_subject = triples[0].subject

    # Build sentence parts
    sentence_parts = []

    # Process triples in order, building a coherent narrative
    for i, t in enumerate(triples):
        clean_obj = clean_value(t.object)
        predicate = t.predicate

        # Build clause based on predicate and context
        if t.subject == main_subject:
            if predicate == "author":
                part = f"{clean_obj} wrote {main_subject}"
            elif predicate == "isbnNumber":
                part = f"with ISBN {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "followedBy":
                part = f"followed by {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "precededBy":
                part = f"preceded by {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "language":
                part = f"in {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "mediaType":
                part = f"in {clean_obj} format"
            elif predicate == "numberOfPages":
                part = f"containing {clean_obj} pages"
            elif predicate == "country":
                part = f"published in {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "publisher":
                part = f"published by {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "genre":
                part = f"a {clean_obj} work"
            elif predicate == "literaryGenre":
                part = f"in the {clean_obj} genre"
            elif predicate == "releaseDate":
                part = f"released on {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "abbreviation":
                part = f"abbreviated as {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "firstPublicationYear":
                part = f"first published in {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "issnNumber":
                part = f"with ISSN {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "codenCode":
                part = f"with CODEN {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "LCCN_number":
                part = f"with LCCN {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "eissnNumber":
                part = f"with eISSN {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "frequency":
                part = f"published {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "academicDiscipline":
                part = f"covering {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "editor":
                part = f"edited by {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "impactFactor":
                part = f"with impact factor {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "oclcNumber":
                part = f"with OCLC {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "libraryofCongressClassification":
                part = f"classified as {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "influencedBy":
                part = f"influenced by {clean_obj}"
            else:
                part = f"{t.subject} {predicate} {clean_obj}"
        else:
            # Handle related subjects (people, organizations, etc.)
            if predicate == "almaMater":
                part = f"{t.subject} attended {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "doctoralAdvisor":
                part = f"{t.subject}'s advisor was {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "nationality":
                part = f"{t.subject} is from {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "residence":
                part = f"{t.subject} resides in {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "birthPlace":
                part = f"{t.subject} was born in {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "deathPlace":
                part = f"{t.subject} died in {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "birthDate":
                part = f"{t.subject} was born on {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "influencedBy":
                part = f"{t.subject} was influenced by {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "notableWork":
                part = f"{t.subject} is known for {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "affiliation":
                part = f"{t.subject} is affiliated with {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "city":
                part = f"{t.subject} is located in {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "state":
                part = f"in {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "headquarter":
                part = f"{t.subject} is headquartered in {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "leader":
                part = f"{t.subject} is led by {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "founder":
                part = f"{t.subject} was founded by {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "parentCompany":
                part = f"{t.subject} is a subsidiary of {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "editor":
                part = f"{t.subject} is edited by {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "publisher":
                part = f"{t.subject} is published by {clean_obj}"
            else:
                part = f"{t.subject} {predicate} {clean_obj}"

        sentence_parts.append(part)

    # Combine all parts into one flowing sentence
    if len(sentence_parts) == 1:
        sentence = sentence_parts[0]
    elif len(sentence_parts) == 2:
        sentence = sentence_parts[0] + ", " + sentence_parts[1]
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(sentence_parts[:-1]) + ", and " + sentence_parts[-1]

    # Ensure proper capitalization and punctuation
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:] + "."

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a written work into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Clean object values - remove quotes from strings
    def clean_value(val: str) -> str:
        if val.startswith('"') and val.endswith('"'):
            return val[1:-1]
        return val

    # Determine the main subject (first triple's subject)
    main_subject = triples[0].subject

    # Collect all info for the main subject
    main_info = []
    related_info = []
    
    for t in triples:
        clean_obj = clean_value(t.object)
        predicate = t.predicate
        
        if t.subject == main_subject:
            # Build clause for main subject
            if predicate == "author":
                clause = f"was written by {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "isbnNumber":
                clause = f"has ISBN {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "followedBy":
                clause = f"is followed by {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "precededBy":
                clause = f"is preceded by {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "language":
                clause = f"is in {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "mediaType":
                clause = f"is in {clean_obj} format"
            elif predicate == "numberOfPages":
                clause = f"has {clean_obj} pages"
            elif predicate == "country":
                clause = f"is from {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "publisher":
                clause = f"is published by {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "genre":
                clause = f"is a {clean_obj} work"
            elif predicate == "literaryGenre":
                clause = f"is in the {clean_obj} genre"
            elif predicate == "releaseDate":
                clause = f"was released on {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "abbreviation":
                clause = f"is abbreviated as {clean_obj}"
            elif predicate == "firstPublicationYear":
                clause = f"was first published in {clean_obj}"
            else:
                clause = f"{predicate}: {clean_obj}"
            
            main_info.append(clause)
        else:
            # Collect related subject info
            related_info.append(f"{t.subject} {predicate} {clean_obj}")
    
    # Build the final sentence
    if not main_info:
        return main_subject + "."
    
    if len(main_info) == 1:
        sentence = f"{main_subject} {main_info[0]}"
    else:
        sentence = f"{main_subject}" + " " + ", ".join(main_info)
    
    # Add related info if present
    if related_info:
        sentence += ". " + ". ".join(related_info)
    
    # Ensure proper capitalization and punctuation
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:] + "."
>>>>>>> REPLACE
